docs.title: Commita
docs.text: Born from a friend's idea and the thought that interfaces for Git aren't nice, Commita's here to revolutionize.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Commita is a framework and a wrapper for Git, you can call it as you wish.

Commita's sole purpose is to simplify Git, starting from the process of commiting, while also featuring self-healing to most of Git Errors.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Quicker Pipeline — Commita simplifies the process of commiting and pushing

  • Self-Healing — Commita is capable of self-healing errors

    • This is a WORK IN PROGRESS! Do not trust Commita for big projects yet!

  • Helpful Commands — Commita features a few pre-defined semantical commit options, with also the idea of allowing you to define your own semantical commits

  • Simple Configurations — Commita creates your own config folder .commita for all your projects, helping you keep your preferred settings to each project you have.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Commita has a installer being worked on, this part can change at any moment!

First, clone this repository:

git clone https://github.com/gabrielEFagundes/commita

Then simply build core.go from this repository to your prefered folder and add it to your PATH.

go build -o /path/to/your/folder/commita.exe core.go # THIS WILL BE CHANGED SOON
  • On windows, you can use path manager
  • On Linux, you can use the following command:
export PATH=$PATH:/path/to/your/folder

Note that this is only temporary, you need to add that same line to your ~/.profile file for permanent changes.

  • On MacOS, add the path to commita into the /etc/paths file.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
Start by setuping your own configurations:

commita config set -u "https://your-repo-url/" -b "your-default-branch"

Then, define your git login for the project:

commita config login --local/--global "your-user" "your-email"

You're ready to Go now!

docs.title: Basic example
docs.text:
commita commit --feat "Adding new feature"

# ✨ feat: Adding new feature

commita commit --type "perf" "Upgrading performance"

# perf: Upgrading performance

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
{
    "defaultBranch": "main",
    "useEmoji": true,
    "useAi": false,
    "url": "",
    "login": {
        "user": "",
        "email": "",
        "abundance": ""
    }
}

Every configuration can be changed from the JSON or with command lines.

Pro tip

You don't have to parse your branch if you don't want to, Commita's default branch on the config file is main.

docs.title: Error handling
docs.text:
commita commit --docs "Updating README.md"

Initializing your repository...             [ OK ]
Adding your remote to the origin...         [ OK ]
Staging your changes...                     [ OK ]
Commiting your changes...                   [ OK ]
Changing to your branch...                  [ OK ]
Pushing your changes...                     [ FATAL! ]
You have changes on your remote that aren't in your local repo!
Would you like me to pull your remote data? (y/n) yes

Done!

docs.text: Examples are coming soon!

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Contributions are welcome! Please read the contributing guide before opening a PR.

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT © Gabriel Ehrat Fagundes

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: First off, thanks for taking the time to contribute! Every bit helps, whether it's fixing a typo or adding a new feature.

docs.title: Before you start
docs.text:
• Check the open issues to see if your idea or bug is already being tracked
  • For big changes, open an issue first to discuss — this avoids wasted effort if the direction doesn't fit the project
  • For small fixes (typos, docs, minor bugs), just open a PR directly

docs.title: Setting up the project
docs.text:
Requirements:

  • Go 1.21+
  • Git

Clone and build:

git clone https://github.com/gabrielEFagundes/commita
cd commita
go mod download
go build -o /path/to/commita/commita.exe core.go

docs.title: Branching
docs.text:
Create a branch from main with the first letters of your username and what the branch does:

git checkout -b gef/some-bug
git checkout -b gef/some-feature
git checkout -b gef/improve-readme

docs.title: Code style
docs.text:
This project follows standard Go conventions, we have to keep it the Go Way!

# format your code before committing
gofmt -w .

# run the linter
go vet ./...

Tip

If you use VS Code, install the Go extension — it formats on save automatically.

docs.title: Commits
docs.text:
Keep commits focused and use clear messages:

feat: add support for X
fix: handle nil pointer in Y
docs: update installation steps
test: add coverage for Z
refactor: simplify X logic

Emojis are optional, you can use them if you want!

docs.title: Submitting a pull request
docs.text:
1. Make sure all tests pass;
  2. Make sure the code is formatted: gofmt -w .
  3. Push your branch and open a PR against main
  4. Fill in the PR description — check out the Template
  5. Wait for review — I'll get back to you as soon as possible

Note

PRs that don't work on my local machine will be put on stand-by until the reason for that is found.

docs.title: Reporting bugs
docs.text:
Open an issue and include:

  • Go version (go version)
  • OS and architecture
  • A minimal example that shows off the bug
  • What you expected vs what actually happened

Check out for the Issue Template

docs.title: Suggesting features
docs.text:
Open an issue with the enhancement label and describe:

  • The problem you're trying to solve
  • How you'd expect the feature to work
  • Any alternatives you've considered

docs.title: License
docs.text: By contributing, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under the same MIT License as the project.
